title: parasenecio praetermissus
intro: Parasenecio praetermissus, also known as the "garden senecio," is a type of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It has medicinal properties due to its alkaloid content, particularly senecionine and juncicine, which have been found to have hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Parasenecio praetermissus include its potential to protect against liver damage, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support liver health and alleviate digestive issues.
